**Assistant:** I've read the Hyperframes docs (intro, quickstart, HTML schema, rendering, prompting, compositions, and the block catalog). Here's how it can optimize your blog2video pipeline:

## What Hyperframes is

An agent-native, HTML-based video framework. You write plain HTML with `data-start`/`data-duration`/`data-track-index` attributes, register a paused GSAP timeline, and its CLI renders deterministic MP4s via headless Chrome. Supports 1080×1920, 30fps, draft/standard/high quality — exactly matching your current specs.

## Why it's interesting for blog2video

Your current pipeline is **narration.md → slide_config.json → HTML slides → Puppeteer screenshots (PNG) → Remotion composites PNG+audio → MP4**. Hyperframes collapses the last three stages into one: **HTML → MP4 directly**, animations included (no PNG intermediate, no Remotion React layer).

More importantly, its **block catalog** fills gaps in your current 6-slide-type system:

| Catalog block | Blog2Video use case |
|---|---|
| `x-post`, `reddit-post` | Citing tweet/reddit sources inline (you already handle tweet inputs) |
| `yt-lower-third` | Brand Intro frame (matches your positioning memo) |
| `data-chart` (NYT-style) | Data-heavy blogs where a static card feels thin |
| `flowchart` | "How X works" content — animated decision trees |
| `logo-outro` | Consistent outro across videos |
| 30+ shader/motion transitions | Replace cuts between your 6 slide types |
| Marker highlights (sweep/circle/burst) | Emphasize key Chinese phrases in 小红书 style |

## Three optimization paths (ranked by value/effort)

**1. Low effort, high value — steal the ideas, keep Remotion.**
Add Remotion components that mimic `x-post`, `data-chart`, `flowchart`, `logo-outro` to your 6 slide types. Add marker-highlight animation on key phrases in existing slides. No migration, just more expressive output.

**2. Medium effort — hybrid.**
Keep Remotion for the narration/audio composition layer (your MiniMax TTS integration is already solid), but use Hyperframes for one or two "hero" slides per video (e.g., data-chart reveal or animated flowchart), embed the resulting MP4 clip back in the Remotion timeline.

**3. High effort — full migration.**
Rewrite the render stage in Hyperframes. Upside: single HTML source of truth, no PNG intermediate, richer animation out of the box. Downside: reimplement MiniMax TTS/subtitle pipeline, port all 6 slide types, lose your current render-all.mjs orchestration. Only worth it if you're hitting Remotion walls, which your current output doesn't suggest.

## My recommendation

Start with **Path 1**: pick 2-3 blocks from the catalog (I'd prioritize `x-post`/`reddit-post` given your Twitter input flow, plus `logo-outro` for brand consistency) and port their visual ideas into new Remotion slide types. That's days of work for meaningfully richer videos.

Want me to prototype one of these as a new Remotion slide type?
